analysis: Chaotic: gap narrowed to 2 points (30 vs 28). Skill now finds 4 deductions totaling 16 pts vs judge_05's 5 deductions totaling 18 pts. Both agree on 1.1.C.a, 2.1.A.a, 3.1.A.a, and 1.3.D.a. judge_05 additionally deducts 1.3.A.a (-2).

Chitchat: skill is now STRICTER than judge_05 (38 vs 42). The anti-leniency framing caused the skill to deduct 3.1.A.a (-4 for multi-question responses) which judge_05 did not. This is a valid call — the tutor does pack 3+ questions per turn repeatedly.
Clueless: identical at 42/46. Both find the same 1.3.A.a + 1.3.D.a deductions.
Overall: the lean skill with bias disclaimers and anchor example produces stricter, more discriminating scores. Mean score dropped from 39.3 (v2) to 36.7 (v3), closer to judge_05's 37.3.
